prompt:
In the implementations/controls/ directory of the ieee-research warehouse, create piezo_walking_motor.py and test_piezo_walking_motor.py.

Background: The Walking Piezoelectric Motor uses the alternating clamping-driving cycle of multiple groups of piezoelectric ceramics to produce macroscopic displacements, similar to a "walking" gait. Typical structure: 4 groups of piezoelectric stacks - left clamping, right clamping, left driving, right driving. Each gait cycle is divided into four phases: left clamping -> left drive pushing -> right clamping -> right drive pushing. Compared with stepper motors, it has a larger stroke, higher thrust, and positioning accuracy up to nanometer level. Representative types: inchworm motor, walker piezo motor.

## piezo_walking_motor.py

### 1. WalkingPiezoMotorPlant class
- Four sets of piezoelectric stack models: left_clamp, right_clamp, left_drive, right_drive
- Each stack uses a simplified hysteresis model (preisach or bouc-wen simplified form)
- State: mover displacement x, velocity v, strain of each stack
- Parameters: Each stack stiffness k_i, damping c_i, piezoelectric coefficient d_i, clamping preload
- gait_cycle(voltages: dict, dt) -> step displacement
- simulate(voltage_sequence, dt) -> displacement-time series
- Key phenomena: clamping drift, creep, temperature effect, step consistency

### 2. WalkingGaitController class
- Four-phase gait generation: Clamp_L -> Drive_L -> Clamp_R -> Drive_R
- Timing control: duration of each phase, overlapping transition zone
- Parameters: driving voltage amplitude, frequency, duty cycle
-Symmetric/asymmetric gait mode switching
- generate_gait(freq, amplitude, phase_delay) -> voltage sequence
- Micro-stepping mode: finely adjust the voltage of each phase to achieve sub-step resolution

### 3. WalkingMotorESO class (extended status observer)
- 4th order ESO: z1 (displacement), z2 (velocity), z3 (acceleration/model uncertainty), z4 (external load disturbance)
- Parameter: Observer bandwidth w0
- fal(e, alpha, delta) nonlinear function
- update(y_meas, voltage_seq) -> status update
- Gait phase awareness: adjust the observer gain according to the current gait phase
- get_estimated_states() -> Full state estimation

### 4. WalkingMotorADRC class
- Integrated: Gait Planner + ESO + Disturbance Compensation + NLSEF
- Parameters: wc (control bandwidth), w0 (observer bandwidth), b0 (control gain matrix)
- Position tracking mode: Given the target displacement, automatically generate a gait sequence
- Force/speed control mode
- Variable step size compensation: adjust the step size online according to the ESO estimation error
- track(target_position, dt) -> Control voltage output sequence
- disturbance_compensation(u, z4_estimate) -> control amount after compensation

### 5. Optional WalkingMotorOptimizer class
- Gait parameter optimization (genetic algorithm or particle swarm)
- Objective function: step consistency + speed smoothness + energy consumption minimization
- optimize_gait(plant, target_speed, n_iter) -> optimal parameter set

## test_piezo_walking_motor.py
- Plant basic function test (single-step/multi-step gait)
- WalkingGaitController four-phase timing verification
- ESO convergence speed test
- Position tracking accuracy test (nano level)
- Sudden load immunity test
- Step consistency comparison (open loop vs ADRC)
- Microstepping resolution test
- Gait optimization convergence test

Technical requirements: Python 3.10+, pure NumPy, type annotations, Chinese documentation
Branch name: feat/walking-piezo-motor
title: Walking Piezoelectric Motor ADRC Control
